steps.title: Set the equations equal and factor
steps.content:
At intersection points, both expressions equal $y$:
$$3x^2 - 14x = x$$
Rearrange to standard form:
$$3x^2 - 15x = 0$$
Factor out $3x$:
$$3x(x - 5) = 0$$
This yields $x = 0$ or $x = 5$.

steps.title: Identify a from the non-zero solution
steps.content: We're given that one intersection is $(0, 0)$. The other intersection point is $(a, a)$, which corresponds to $x = 5$ from our factored solutions. Therefore, $a = 5$.
